methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Diode is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Davide Asnaghi and Lenny Khazan that has raised $10M+. The relevant peer universe comprises nine private robotics companies—including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics—alongside one public comparable (277810.KQ). However, the private peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage datapoints with comparable valuations to support a traditional peer-median approach.

Given data constraints, stage-progression analysis provides the primary valuation signal. Using the $10M total raised as a proxy base value and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $25M. This methodology is appropriate for early-stage companies where fundraising velocity often tracks with value creation within a stage cohort.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $21M to $29M, centered on $25M. The median estimate rests on a single method, which represents a material limitation. Key uncertainties include the absence of tightly-comparable private peers at equivalent stage and valuation tier, limiting cross-validation, and the reliance on a standard multiplier that may not reflect Diode's specific market position or capital efficiency. Additional comps or trailing metrics (revenue, unit economics) would materially strengthen conviction in this range.
